content: Architecture restructured from coaching-first (v1) to data-foundation-first (v3). Build order: verified data → risk engine → events → backtesting → AI review → forensics → scoring → rewards → ML. Rationale: coaching without verified data produces generic advice; data foundation enables every downstream feature to be grounded in actual trading performance.

Prop Shield selected as first revenue feature. Pricing: Mentor $29/mo, Trader $99/mo. Rationale: prop traders have acute daily pain around rule tracking (daily loss limits, trailing drawdown). Concrete rules-based feature with clear value prop vs RAG coaching which is harder to monetize immediately.

Phase 1 (Trading Data Foundation) completed 2026-05-13. Deliverables: ORM with 100+ field trade model, CRUD endpoints, bulk import, analytics service, 17 ICT-specific enums, Tradovate fill sync, broker credential encryption, 5 Alembic migrations.

Phase 3 (Prop Shield) completed 2026-05-13. Deliverables: rule engine with distance-to-breach, lockout state machine (warning→soft→hard→reset), 5 prop firm presets (Apex, TopStep, FTMO, E2T, MFF), Stripe billing integration, risk dashboard UI with rule gauges, Discord webhook alerts.

Cross-cutting changes (touching wiki/ + api/ + app/) must ride in a single PR. Wiki is the index and source of truth — always start there when in doubt. Post-implementation reconciliation is mandatory: update architecture docs after code changes.

Track C (Business & Operations, phases 11-16) eliminated as separate track in v3 restructure. Compliance requirements now embedded per-phase with specific gates: Phase 3 (advisory disclaimer), Phase 5 (backtesting disclaimers), Phase 6 (content licensing, not financial advice), Phase 9 (full compliance review, approved/forbidden language), Phase 11 (RIA determination).

NeuroCore (hybrid retrieval: BM25 + semantic + entity) is a domain-specific product feature that stays in api/. Conductor memory.py handles project-level decisions, learnings, patterns, and incidents. These are separate concerns — memory.py is for orchestration intelligence, NeuroCore is for trading knowledge retrieval.

Monorepo consolidation completed 2026-05-02. Three repos (neurospect-wiki, neurospect-api, neurospect-app) merged into single neurospect/ directory via git filter-repo with history preservation. Pre-merge tips tagged pre-monorepo-snapshot. Phase 2 cleanup (Render/Cloudflare dashboard reconfig, old repo archival) pending.
